channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Balancing Career and Family: Challenges in Modern Society
item.description: The article explores the ongoing struggle between pursuing marriage and career advancement, highlighting how modern societal pressures force individuals to make difficult choices. Economic challenges,...

item.title: 19-Year-Old Arrested for Online Child Sexual Abuse
item.description: A 19-year-old suspect was arrested for allegedly engaging in online child sexual abuse, according to a report by ABS-CBN News. The incident highlights the growing concerns about cybercrime involving m...

item.title: Liberty Beauty Advent Calendar 2026: Product Lineup and Details Revealed
item.description: The Liberty beauty advent calendar for 2026 has been unveiled, featuring 29 products including full-sized items from high-end brands like Sunday Riley, MZ Skin, and Augustinus Bader. The calendar, pri...

item.title: Mumbai and Pune face severe flooding and transportation disruptions from heavy monsoons
item.description: Heavy monsoon rains have caused widespread flooding and infrastructure damage across Mumbai and Pune, disrupting daily life and transportation. The Mumbai Metropolitan Region and Pune experienced land...

item.title: FIFA suspends Balogun's red card ban following Trump intervention in World Cup
item.description: FIFA has suspended the one-match ban on USA striker Folarin Balogun for a year after a direct phone call from US President Donald Trump to FIFA president Gianni Infantino. The decision allows Balogun ...

item.title: Canada's Prime Minister Joins NATO Summit Amid Ongoing U.S.-Russia Tensions and Defense Spending Goals
item.description: Prime Minister Justin Trudeau (Carney) is attending a NATO summit in Ankara, where leaders will discuss defense modernization, Ukraine support, and countering Russian aggression. The meeting comes ami...

item.title: ABC's Insiders podcast sees guest changes and political shifts

item.title: Kenya: Missing Files Cause Delay in Arraignment of Keumbu Violence Suspects

item.title: Tanzania Aims to Eliminate Cholera by 2026/27 with New Health Initiatives
item.description: The Tanzanian government has directed regional and council health officials to eliminate cholera outbreaks by 2026/27 as part of the 'Zero Cholera Tanzania' initiative. Minister Prof Riziki Shemdoe hi...

item.title: Comcast’s Sky Acquires ITV in Major Shift for UK Broadcasting Landscape
item.description: Comcast’s Sky has agreed to acquire ITV's broadcast channels and streaming service for £1.6 billion, creating a dominant player in the UK television market. This merger combines Britain's largest free...

item.description: The Durban July Fashion Awards, held at Greyville Racecourse, celebrated South Africa's fashion creativity with a Country Allure theme. Over 150 participants competed across four categories, showcasin...

item.title: Nollywood Actress Converts to Christianity After Nine Years of Illness
item.description: Nollywood actress Halima Abubakar has publicly announced her conversion from Islam to Christianity, attributing her recovery from a mysterious illness that kept her out of public life for nearly nine ...

item.title: Ongoing Ethnic Cleansing in Gaza and International Response

item.title: Day 1 of Sara Duterte's Impeachment Trial: Prosecution Claims Victory Amid Mixed Outcomes
item.description: The first day of Vice President Sara Duterte's impeachment trial on July 6, 2026, saw the prosecution claim a 'victory' despite mixed rulings from the Senate impeachment court. Prosecutors Jinky Luist...

item.title: Burna Boy Defends Luxury Car Purchase Amid Public Criticism
item.description: Nigerian singer Burna Boy faced widespread criticism after reports surfaced that he spent KSh 839 million on a custom Bugatti Chiron VXX Special Edition. The luxury vehicle, valued as one of the most ...

item.title: UEFA Criticizes FIFA's Decision to Suspend Balogun's Red Card
item.description: UEFA has strongly criticized FIFA's decision to suspend Folarin Balogun's red-card ban following U.S. President Donald Trump's intervention. The African-American striker was sent off for a tackle duri...

item.description: The Tanzanian government has emphasized the need for cooperative societies to enhance accountability, adopt digital technologies, and increase youth and women's participation to foster a peaceful and ...

item.title: Spacex President Announces Stock Donation to Trump's Accounts
item.description: The article discusses the announcement by Elon Musk, CEO of SpaceX, regarding a stock donation to Donald Trump's personal and campaign accounts. Musk, who has a history of public support for Trump, pl...

item.title: Former President Zuma faces third attempt to overturn court order for R28.9m legal fees repayment
item.description: Former South African President Jacob Zuma is set to face his third attempt to overturn a court order requiring him to repay R28.9m in legal fees funded by taxpayers. The Constitutional Court has been ...

item.title: NSW Considers Banning Russian 'Z' Symbol as Hate Symbol
item.description: The New South Wales government is examining whether the Russian 'Z' symbol, associated with pro-Russian activists and the invasion of Ukraine, should be classified as a hate symbol. Ukrainian communit...
